{{Not to be confused with|March 9|qq=March 9}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|qq=}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|qq=}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|qq=|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|qq=|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|qq=|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|qq=|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|qq=|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|qq=|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|qid=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|qq=|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|qq=|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|qq=|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839181]] Wikidata name: 9th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 9|qq=March 9}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1211|1211]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Jōgen (Kamakura period)|Shōgen]] to [[:en:Kenryaku|Kenryaku]]. * [[:en:Kōka|Kōka]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1847|1847]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1851|1851]] [[:en:April 10|April 10]]) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * [[:en:Ansei|Ansei]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1856|1856]] [[:en:April 13|April 13]]) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1868|1868]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with [[:en:Saigō Takamori|Saigō Takamori]], appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1606|1606]] [[:en:April 16|April 16]]) – [[:en:Nanbu Shigenao|Nanbu Shigenao]], the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died [[:en:1664|1664]]). * [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 20th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1735|1735]] [[:en:April 1|April 1]]) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died [[:en:1808|1808]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kangen|Kangen]] 6th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1200|1200]] [[:en:April 23|April 23]]) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died [[:en:1130|1130]]). * [[:en:Genki|Genki]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1571|1571]] [[:en:April 3|April 3]]) – [[:en:Tsukahara Bokuden|Tsukahara Bokuden]], a master of Kenjutsu (died [[:en:1489|1489]]). * [[:en:Kan'ei|Kan'ei]] 11th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1634|1634]] [[:en:April 6|April 6]]) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died [[:en:1555|1555]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}

re-designated as the Konoei.

== Deaths == * {{ill|Kangen|en|Kangen|WD=Q1194187|lt=Kangen}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1200|en|1200|WD=Q5224|lt=1200}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died {{ill|1130|en|1130|WD=Q19562|lt=1130}}). * {{ill|Genki|en|Genki|WD=Q21501757|lt=Genki}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1571|en|1571|WD=Q6666|lt=1571}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}) – {{ill|Tsukahara Bokuden|en|Tsukahara Bokuden|WD=Q767236|lt=Tsukahara Bokuden}}, a master of Kenjutsu (died {{ill|1489|en|1489|WD=Q6665|lt=1489}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1634|en|1634|WD=Q6872|lt=1634}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died {{ill|1555|en|1555|WD=Q6579|lt=1555}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839181]] Wikidata name: 9th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 9|qq=March 9}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1211|en|1211|WD=Q5251|lt=1211}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|en|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194389|lt=Shōgen}} to {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku}}. * {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1847|en|1847|WD=Q7644|lt=1847}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1851|en|1851|WD=Q7656|lt=1851}} {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|WD=Q2502|lt=April 10}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856}} {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}}) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with {{ill|Saigō Takamori|en|Saigō Takamori|WD=Q310462|lt=Saigō Takamori}}, appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1606|en|1606|WD=Q6747|lt=1606}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – {{ill|Nanbu Shigenao|en|Nanbu Shigenao|WD=Q6962458|lt=Nanbu Shigenao}}, the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 20th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1735|en|1735|WD=Q6984|lt=1735}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died {{ill|1808|en|1808|WD=Q6914|lt=1808}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kangen|en|Kangen|WD=Q1194187|lt=Kangen}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1200|en|1200|WD=Q5224|lt=1200}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died {{ill|1130|en|1130|WD=Q19562|lt=1130}}). * {{ill|Genki|en|Genki|WD=Q21501757|lt=Genki}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1571|en|1571|WD=Q6666|lt=1571}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}) – {{ill|Tsukahara Bokuden|en|Tsukahara Bokuden|WD=Q767236|lt=Tsukahara Bokuden}}, a master of Kenjutsu (died {{ill|1489|en|1489|WD=Q6665|lt=1489}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1634|en|1634|WD=Q6872|lt=1634}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died {{ill|1555|en|1555|WD=Q6579|lt=1555}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 9 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月9日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 9일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 9 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月初九]][[zh:三月初九]]
{{Not to be confused with|March 9|qq=March 9}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月初九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 9일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 9''' is the 9th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1211|en|1211|WD=Q5251|lt=1211}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|en|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194389|lt=Shōgen}} to {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku}}. * {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1847|en|1847|WD=Q7644|lt=1847}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – A scholarly institute for the court nobles was established in front of the Imperial Palace's Kenshun-mon in Kyoto, which is regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1851|en|1851|WD=Q7656|lt=1851}} {{ill|April 10|en|April 10|WD=Q2502|lt=April 10}}) – The Tokugawa shogunate issued the Kabunakama Saikōrei. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|WD=Q1327259|lt=Ansei}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1856|en|1856|WD=Q7676|lt=1856}} {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}}) – The practice of the "fumi-e" (stepping on images) at the port cities of Nagasaki, Shimoda, and other open ports was abolished. * {{ill|Meiji Gannen|ja|明治元年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Gannen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – At Shunpū Castle, Yamauchi Yōtoku met with {{ill|Saigō Takamori|en|Saigō Takamori|WD=Q310462|lt=Saigō Takamori}}, appealing for submission and apology to save the Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1606|en|1606|WD=Q6747|lt=1606}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – {{ill|Nanbu Shigenao|en|Nanbu Shigenao|WD=Q6962458|lt=Nanbu Shigenao}}, the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 20th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1735|en|1735|WD=Q6984|lt=1735}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died {{ill|1808|en|1808|WD=Q6914|lt=1808}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kangen|en|Kangen|WD=Q1194187|lt=Kangen}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1200|en|1200|WD=Q5224|lt=1200}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23}}) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died {{ill|1130|en|1130|WD=Q19562|lt=1130}}). * {{ill|Genki|en|Genki|WD=Q21501757|lt=Genki}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1571|en|1571|WD=Q6666|lt=1571}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3}}) – {{ill|Tsukahara Bokuden|en|Tsukahara Bokuden|WD=Q767236|lt=Tsukahara Bokuden}}, a master of Kenjutsu (died {{ill|1489|en|1489|WD=Q6665|lt=1489}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1634|en|1634|WD=Q6872|lt=1634}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|WD=Q2506|lt=April 6}}) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died {{ill|1555|en|1555|WD=Q6579|lt=1555}}).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|8|3|10|2|9|4|3|0309|qq=}}

to 1868年|1=1868|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7717}} ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} {{ill|April Tokugawa family. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – The soldiers protecting the emperor, numbering 10,000 from the Satsuma, Chōshū, and Tosa domains, were re-designated as the Konoei.
== Births == * {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1606|en|1606|WD=Q6747|lt=1606}} {{ill|April 16|en|April 16|WD=Q2519|lt=April 16}}) – {{ill|Nanbu Shigenao|en|Nanbu Shigenao|WD=Q6962458|lt=Nanbu Shigenao}}, the 2nd lord of the Morioka Domain (died {{ill|1664|en|1664|WD=Q7581|lt=1664}}). * {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 20th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1735|en|1735|WD=Q6984|lt=1735}} {{ill|April 1|en|April 1|WD=Q2510|lt=April 1}}) – {{ill|Katō Chien|ja|加藤千蔭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katō Chien|1=Katō Chikage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1293828}}, a Kokugaku scholar and waka poet (died {{ill|1808|en|1808|WD=Q6914|lt=1808}}).

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|WD=Q96759844|1=taian}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku|1=Kenryaku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1211|en|1211|WD=Q5251|lt=1211|1=1211}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|en|Jōgen (Kamakura period)|WD=Q1194389|lt=Shōgen|1=Jōgen (Kamakura period)}} to {{ill|Kenryaku|en|Kenryaku|WD=Q1194260|lt=Kenryaku|1=Kenryaku}}. * {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|WD=Q1325317|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka}} 4th year {{ill|Gakushūin|ja|学習院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakushūin|1=Gakushūin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11448874}}. * {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei|1=Kaei}} 4th year == Deaths == * {{ill|Kangen|en|Kangen|qid=Q1194187|lt=Kangen|1=Kangen}} 6th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1200|en|1200|qid=Q5224|lt=1200|1=1200}} {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|qid=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}}) – {{ill|Zhu Xi|ja|朱子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhu Xi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 朱熹|1=Zhu Xi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9397}}, a Confucian scholar from the Southern Song, regarded as the founder of Zhu Xi's school of Confucianism (birth year unknown, died {{ill|1130|en|1130|qid=Q19562|lt=1130|1=1130}}). * {{ill|Genki|en|Genki|qid=Q21501757|lt=Genki|1=Genki}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1571|en|1571|qid=Q6666|lt=1571|1=1571}} {{ill|April 3|en|April 3|qid=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}}) – {{ill|Tsukahara Bokuden|en|Tsukahara Bokuden|qid=Q767236|lt=Tsukahara Bokuden|1=Tsukahara Bokuden}}, a master of Kenjutsu (died {{ill|1489|en|1489|qid=Q6665|lt=1489|1=1489}}). * {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}} 11th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1634|en|1634|qid=Q6872|lt=1634|1=1634}} {{ill|April 6|en|April 6|qid=Q2506|lt=April 6|1=April 6}}) – {{ill|Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja|初代大橋宗桂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsudai Ōhashi Sōketsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大橋宗桂 (初代)|1=Ohashi Sokei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11436582}}, a shogi master and the first Meijin (died {{ill|1555|en|1555|qid=Q6579|lt=1555|1=1555}}).
